整套首页
unit_06 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_06

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 430 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC DOUBLE
Any account of experimental music in the United States that (i)__________ the predominantly African American bebop and free jazz movements is (ii)__________, since this body of music constitutes what is arguably the most influential African experimental music in the decades following the Second World War.Any account of experimental music in the United States that (i)__________ the predominantly African American bebop and free jazz movements is (ii)__________, since this body of music constitutes what is arguably the most influential African experimental music in the decades following the Second World War.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　neglects; problematic
underappreciatedserveexperimental
underappreciated: standard unit headword | serve: standard unit headword | experimental: standard unit headword
#2 TC TRIPLE
Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; A staleness
lucrativeeruditeesteem
lucrative: standard unit headword | erudite: standard unit headword | esteem: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
In a view of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.In a view of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　physical activity; detract from; augmented
contributenotecounteract
contribute: standard unit headword | note: standard unit headword | counteract: standard unit headword
#4 SE
His own writing style was __________: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.His own writing style was __________: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.
答案：B, D　audacious; striking
tartjuxtaposition
tart: standard unit headword | juxtaposition: standard unit headword
#5 SE
The book captures the __________ of several politicians who spoke publicly of old-time virtues in order to mask private vices.The book captures the __________ of several politicians who spoke publicly of old-time virtues in order to mask private vices.
答案：B, F　dissemblance; duplicity
opportunismprobity
opportunism: standard unit headword | probity: standard unit headword
#6 SE
Many theorists believe that measures to prevent industrial pollution necessarily increase production costs, but several recent reports document innovations that __________ environmental harm while also delivering economic benefits.Many theorists believe that measures to prevent industrial pollution necessarily increase production costs, but several recent reports document innovations that __________ environmental harm while also delivering economic benefits.
答案：D, E　mitigate; curtail
divulgedisclosemitigate
divulge: standard unit headword | disclose: standard unit headword | mitigate: standard unit headword
#7 TC TRIPLE
When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　ambiguity; misleading; veracity
caseconcise
case: standard unit headword | concise: standard unit headword
#8 TC TRIPLE
The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine
traumaticriveting
traumatic: standard unit headword | riveting: standard unit headword
#9 TC DOUBLE
In those days, most county prosecutors' offices were extremely (i) __________ operations, with few meaningful contacts beyond the county's borders. Thus, it is (ii) __________ that Kaus, during her tenure as county prosecutor, reached all the way to the nation's capital in order to overhaul the office.In those days, most county prosecutors' offices were extremely (i) __________ operations, with few meaningful contacts beyond the county's borders. Thus, it is (ii) __________ that Kaus, during her tenure as county prosecutor, reached all the way to the nation's capital in order to overhaul the office.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　insular; noteworthy
noteworthytenure
noteworthy: standard unit headword | tenure: standard unit headword
#10 SE
Attempts to calm down the __________ toddler proved futile: he fidgeted, hollered, and whined during the entire three-hour plane flight.Attempts to calm down the __________ toddler proved futile: he fidgeted, hollered, and whined during the entire three-hour plane flight.
答案：B, F　recalcitrant; restive
impassivebefuddle
impassive: standard unit headword | befuddle: standard unit headword
#11 TC TRIPLE
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce
ambiguoushearten
ambiguous: standard unit headword | hearten: standard unit headword
#12 TC DOUBLE
It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i) __________ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii) __________, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i) __________ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii) __________, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　depleted; overharvested
speculatedeplete
speculate: standard unit headword | deplete: standard unit headword
#13 TC TRIPLE
With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)__________ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)__________ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk
ponderousaggrandize
ponderous: standard unit headword | aggrandize: standard unit headword
#14 TC TRIPLE
Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　reverence for; exemplary; irreconcilable
exoticfeckless
exotic: standard unit headword | feckless: standard unit headword
#15 SE
The space travels described in science fiction stories were always epic adventures, and, when compared with those travels, current journeys in space are much smaller and the tasks performed are far more __________.The space travels described in science fiction stories were always epic adventures, and, when compared with those travels, current journeys in space are much smaller and the tasks performed are far more __________.
答案：B, D　quotidian; prosaic
epicprosaic
epic: standard unit headword | prosaic: standard unit headword
#16 TC TRIPLE
Transportation maps of Alaska are (i)__________ in large part by what they (ii)__________: lines identifying roads and railroads. With a single track bisecting the state and a handful of spokes to the east and south, Alaska is notable for its (iii)__________ of transportation options.Transportation maps of Alaska are (i)__________ in large part by what they (ii)__________: lines identifying roads and railroads. With a single track bisecting the state and a handful of spokes to the east and south, Alaska is notable for its (iii)__________ of transportation options.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　characterized; lack; dearth
notableelucidate
notable: standard unit headword | elucidate: standard unit headword
#17 TC SINGLE
The identity of hominid remains found in a cave in the Altai Mountains was __________ until Paabo and his colleagues ended the speculation by showing that DNA sequences indicated the bones belonged to Neanderthals.The identity of hominid remains found in a cave in the Altai Mountains was __________ until Paabo and his colleagues ended the speculation by showing that DNA sequences indicated the bones belonged to Neanderthals.
答案：B　conjectural
cave in
cave in: standard unit headword
#18 TC SINGLE
Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even __________: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even __________: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.
答案：E　inevitable
instantaneous
instantaneous: standard unit headword
#19 TC TRIPLE
The current organization of agricultural production is certainly not (i) __________ addressing the problem of world hunger. Much of the corn and soy grown in the world feeds cattle, pigs and chickens being raised for meat. This despite the (ii) __________ that is (iii) __________ such a process: two to five times more grain is required to produce the same number of calories in meat as can be obtained through the direct consumption of grain.The current organization of agricultural production is certainly not (i) __________ addressing the problem of world hunger. Much of the corn and soy grown in the world feeds cattle, pigs and chickens being raised for meat. This despite the (ii) __________ that is (iii) __________ such a process: two to five times more grain is required to produce the same number of calories in meat as can be obtained through the direct consumption of grain.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　optimal for; inefficiency; inherent in
inherentfecundity
inherent: standard unit headword | fecundity: standard unit headword
#20 TC SINGLE
In the last two hundred years, the practice of archaeology has changed greatly, from digging up ancient artifacts for use by wealthy individuals as art objects to analyzing the detritus of everyday life in the laboratory, and thus from __________ to data collection.In the last two hundred years, the practice of archaeology has changed greatly, from digging up ancient artifacts for use by wealthy individuals as art objects to analyzing the detritus of everyday life in the laboratory, and thus from __________ to data collection.
答案：D　treasure hunting
artifact
artifact: standard unit headword

The professor frequently reiterated a basic assumption behind the experimental method—namely, that the outcome of the experiment is always (i)__________. The hypothesis can never assume the experiment’s results, in other words, but instead must (ii)__________ their appearance.The professor frequently reiterated a basic assumption behind the experimental method—namely, that the outcome of the experiment is always (i)__________. The hypothesis can never assume the experiment’s results, in other words, but instead must (ii)__________ their appearance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undetermined; await
experimentalreiterate

答案：D　outstrips

答案：D, F　placate; appease

答案：D　self-effacing

Blank 2
答案：C, F　obdurate; malleable

答案：A, B　acrimony; bitterness

Blank 2

答案：A　thrift
bold

But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
tilt
tilt: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable

Researchers concluded that thinking of reasons interferes with people's ability to access gut-level reactions that are crucial to sound decision making.

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 2

#47 TC TRIPLE
For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　unorthodox; aloof from; strongly ahistorical
make sense
make sense: standard unit headword

答案：B　pervasive
cataclysmic
cataclysmic: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　shackle; stifle

True science, we now know, advances human knowledge by (ii) __________ ultimate causes and focusing instead on the testing of empirical hypotheses.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　jettisons; ignoring

dictum: standard unit headword
#51 TC TRIPLE
Genetic researchers have recently suggested that our political leanings may be determined by our DNA, a view that tends to undermine our reflective self-flattery. We (i)__________ the idea that personal politics are entirely (ii)__________. The genetic explanation for ideology (iii)__________ our belief that we are persuaded only by rational arguments.Genetic researchers have recently suggested that our political leanings may be determined by our DNA, a view that tends to undermine our reflective self-flattery. We (i)__________ the idea that personal politics are entirely (ii)__________. The genetic explanation for ideology (iii)__________ our belief that we are persuaded only by rational arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　revel in; deliberative; defies

recoil: standard unit headword | serve: standard unit headword

答案：A　unctuous
unctuous
unctuous: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　innocuous; evasive
query
query: standard unit headword
#54 SE

答案：D, F　transient; unstable
susceptible
susceptible: standard unit headword

If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.
答案：B　augmenting

winnow: standard unit headword
#56 SE

答案：D, F　capture; secure

Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)__________. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains - even the air we breathe - seem the norm and therefore (iii)__________.Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)__________. Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)__________. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains - even the air we breathe - seem the norm and therefore (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　atypical; permanent; eternal

Each of these circumstances causes older bees to become cognitively (ii) __________, while juvenile bees, by contrast, exhibit a faster age-related cognitive decline.In aging bees, cognitive decline is (i) __________ under certain social conditions--for example, when the colony divides by swarming or when foragers or young nurse bees are removed from the colony. Each of these circumstances causes older bees to become cognitively (ii) __________, while juvenile bees, by contrast, exhibit a faster age-related cognitive decline.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　reversed; younger

juvenile: standard unit headword

Blank 2

While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　Demolishing; overshadowed; insignificant

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　expensive; useless; serve no practical purpose

答案：B, E　exalt; valorize

It is hard to write a good novel that takes a strong stand on a social issue, although there are a few novelists, Zola and Norris for example, who do manage to (i) __________ the often circumscribing effects of (ii) __________.In writing about environmental issues, the author takes on some special artistic challenges. Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
inherent
inherent: standard unit headword

These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. tenure: standard unit headword
#106 TC TRIPLE
Publishers, publicists, and broadcasters love anniversaries, those occasions when historical events become (i)__________ in (ii)__________ culture of celebration. On such occasions patriotic sentiment and national pride are wrapped in the panoply of history to manufacture a mythical past that is serviceable for public (iii)__________.Publishers, publicists, and broadcasters love anniversaries, those occasions when historical events become (i)__________ in (ii)__________ culture of celebration. On such occasions patriotic sentiment and national pride are wrapped in the panoply of history to manufacture a mythical past that is serviceable for public (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　marketable artifacts; a commercial; consumption
artifact
artifact: standard unit headword
#107 TC SINGLE
The modest but functional new wing finally gives the museum the __________ to serve its visitors properly, including multiple entrances to eliminate the lines that used to snake around the building.The modest but functional new wing finally gives the museum the __________ to serve its visitors properly, including multiple entrances to eliminate the lines that used to snake around the building.
答案：B　wherewithal
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#108 TC DOUBLE
Not only is the field of behavioral genetics strewn with (i)__________ finding, but even among those findings managed to survive, many have turned out to be (ii)__________ a very restricted class of cases.Not only is the field of behavioral genetics strewn with (i)__________ finding, but even among those findings managed to survive, many have turned out to be (ii)__________ a very restricted class of cases.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　repudiated; germane to
case
case: standard unit headword
#109 SE
The Great Lakes wolf is a __________, stumping scientists as to whether it is a subspecies of the gray wolf or a distinct species.The Great Lakes wolf is a __________, stumping scientists as to whether it is a subspecies of the gray wolf or a distinct species.
答案：B, F　riddle; conundrum
conundrum
conundrum: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)__________ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)__________ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)__________.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)__________ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)__________ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data
tenure
tenure: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
Hyana Kusiemko and her colleagues speculate that the (i)__________ support among low-income workers for increases in the minimum wage is a form of last-place aversion: people who are in a marginally better position than the worst off seek to (ii)__________ to distinguish themselves from those in last place.Hyana Kusiemko and her colleagues speculate that the (i)__________ support among low-income workers for increases in the minimum wage is a form of last-place aversion: people who are in a marginally better position than the worst off seek to (ii)__________ to distinguish themselves from those in last place.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　tepid; retain their ability
speculate
speculate: standard unit headword
#112 TC SINGLE
Scientists could not build on each other's work if they could not trust the published work of their colleagues; thus progress in science depends on the __________ of practicing scientists.Scientists could not build on each other's work if they could not trust the published work of their colleagues; thus progress in science depends on the __________ of practicing scientists.
答案：C　probity
probity
probity: standard unit headword
#113 SE
Jaime Javier Rodriguez notes that popular art forms often conceal a daunting complexity: frequently what appears __________ becomes intricate and challenging when closely examined.Jaime Javier Rodriguez notes that popular art forms often conceal a daunting complexity: frequently what appears __________ becomes intricate and challenging when closely examined.
答案：B, F　canned; formulaic
note
note: standard unit headword
#114 TC TRIPLE
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; apt; impugned
case
case: standard unit headword
#115 TC DOUBLE
Regardless of the putative decline of (i)__________ in contemporary culture, such (ii)__________ remains essential to civil discourse. It allows people to avoid embarrassing or maligning an adversary, and it serves as a form of irony that draw attention to a problem that can scarcely be exaggerated.Regardless of the putative decline of (i)__________ in contemporary culture, such (ii)__________ remains essential to civil discourse. It allows people to avoid embarrassing or maligning an adversary, and it serves as a form of irony that draw attention to a problem that can scarcely be exaggerated.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　understatement; restraint
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#116 TC SINGLE
Of all her works, this play is the most dependent on the dramatic conventions of the author’s day; it was both the least __________ of her plays and the most commercially successful.Of all her works, this play is the most dependent on the dramatic conventions of the author’s day; it was both the least __________ of her plays and the most commercially successful.
答案：A　experimental
lucrativeexperimental
lucrative: standard unit headword | experimental: standard unit headword
#117 SE
Reuters are not against __________ noteworthy architecture but suggests a cap on the amount to be protected at any one time: if you want to protect another ancient building, one should come off the list.Reuters are not against __________ noteworthy architecture but suggests a cap on the amount to be protected at any one time: if you want to protect another ancient building, one should come off the list.
答案：A, F　preserving; safeguarding
noteworthy
noteworthy: standard unit headword
#118 TC TRIPLE
Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Handi Murdd is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale’s creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy’s nonfiction.Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Handi Murdd is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale’s creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy’s nonfiction.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　a preference for; suppress; didacticism
epic
epic: standard unit headword
#119 TC DOUBLE
For private fossil collectors, a fossil's value is (i) __________ the object itself. For scientists, however, the value of a fossil is usually (ii) __________. Most paleontological research focuses not only on the fossil itself, but also on the fossil's location, the kind of rock it was found in, its position in the historical sequence, and how the fossil is positioned relative to other fossils.For private fossil collectors, a fossil's value is (i) __________ the object itself. For scientists, however, the value of a fossil is usually (ii) __________. Most paleontological research focuses not only on the fossil itself, but also on the fossil's location, the kind of rock it was found in, its position in the historical sequence, and how the fossil is positioned relative to other fossils.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　inherent in; contextual
inherent
inherent: standard unit headword
#120 TC SINGLE
Even those observers who are the most __________ about genetic privacy issues would have to concede that genetic discrimination is rare: there have only been two cases of any notoriety.Even those observers who are the most __________ about genetic privacy issues would have to concede that genetic discrimination is rare: there have only been two cases of any notoriety.
答案：B　zealous
case
case: standard unit headword
#121 SE
As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her __________; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her __________; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.
答案：B, D　humility; modesty
noteworthy
noteworthy: standard unit headword

Much of the newspaper's readership found it hard to (i)__________ the allegations of venality laid against the superintendent, since these readers were still (ii)__________ the superintendent's reputation for (iii)__________. The superintendent's subsequent vindication will have come as no surprise to them.Much of the newspaper's readership found it hard to (i)__________ the allegations of venality laid against the superintendent, since these readers were still (ii)__________ the superintendent's reputation for (iii)__________. The superintendent's subsequent vindication will have come as no surprise to them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　credit; swayed by; probity

In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i)__________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii)__________ a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i)__________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii)__________ a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　promotes; constraints

case: standard unit headword

答案：A　flout
prose

Although in his new book he tends to repeat himself like a __________ uncle, McHughen makes a persuasive case for the safety of tinkering with genes to create new foods.Although in his new book he tends to repeat himself like a __________ uncle, McHughen makes a persuasive case for the safety of tinkering with genes to create new foods.
答案：D, E　garrulous; loquacious

Blank 2

答案：A, D, H　appreciated; kept them at a distance; contributing to

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　disappointed in; suffer

Some ambitious lawyers are willing to work on Supreme Court cases without charge in an effort to gain __________; they believe that this increased cachet will help them succeed in the future.Some ambitious lawyers are willing to work on Supreme Court cases without charge in an effort to gain __________; they believe that this increased cachet will help them succeed in the future.
答案：C　prestige

case: standard unit headword
#129 SE
Although the employees’ union and company management, entering into contract negotiations, both issued statements encouraging __________, acrimony between the two sides continued unabated.Although the employees’ union and company management, entering into contract negotiations, both issued statements encouraging __________, acrimony between the two sides continued unabated.
答案：D, E　civility; comity

#130 TC TRIPLE
Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)__________ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)__________ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)__________ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)__________ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)__________ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)__________ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　attend disproportionately to; responsiveness to; mask
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#131 TC DOUBLE
Readers may initially be irked by the book's apparent (i) __________ but, once immersed in the author's prose, they may come to regard the work's (ii) __________ as an asset.Readers may initially be irked by the book's apparent (i) __________ but, once immersed in the author's prose, they may come to regard the work's (ii) __________ as an asset.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　aimlessness; discursions

#132 SE
Flash floods are common in desert regions and were widespread before the evolution of trees and woodland soils; in contrast, flash floods are __________ in woodlands, where floodwaters, impeded by trees, form ponds.Flash floods are common in desert regions and were widespread before the evolution of trees and woodland soils; in contrast, flash floods are __________ in woodlands, where floodwaters, impeded by trees, form ponds.
答案：E, F　mitigated; abated

Although thawing permafrost often contributes to carbon emissions by releasing methane, active methanotrophs (a specialized group of microorganisms) utilize methane, __________ declines in net emissions under extended periods of warm temperature.Although thawing permafrost often contributes to carbon emissions by releasing methane, active methanotrophs (a specialized group of microorganisms) utilize methane, __________ declines in net emissions under extended periods of warm temperature.
答案：B　effecting

contribute: standard unit headword

The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　get; incidental to; powerful antidote to

Scholars have marveled over the (i) __________ that Shakespeare displays in his works, noting that such broad learning is all the more remarkable given that books were relatively (ii) __________ in Shakespeare's time.Scholars have marveled over the (i) __________ that Shakespeare displays in his works, noting that such broad learning is all the more remarkable given that books were relatively (ii) __________ in Shakespeare's time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　erudition; scarce

The __________ of chicken in the Western diet is notable: we now eat 50 billion chickens a year, easily making chicken the largest source of animal protein on the planet.The __________ of chicken in the Western diet is notable: we now eat 50 billion chickens a year, easily making chicken the largest source of animal protein on the planet.
答案：D　ubiquity

#138 SE
All Shaker furniture implies __________ humanism in design: the Shakers made objects that look like objects, following a nonhuman law of design and rejecting the unconscious self-flattery inherent in making anthropomorphic objects.All Shaker furniture implies __________ humanism in design: the Shakers made objects that look like objects, following a nonhuman law of design and rejecting the unconscious self-flattery inherent in making anthropomorphic objects.
答案：A, B　a rejection of; a liberation from

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　mitigated; vulnerable

答案：E　co-opted

答案：B, C　conceded; acknowledged

答案：A　livid

Although this quality of Marson's poetry struck Sherlock as a sign of her emotional generosity (he remarked on her great "love of her homeland and its people"), Sherlock's (ii) __________ was not (iii) __________: he found some of Marson's poems excessive in their emotionality.Writing about Jamaican poet Una Marson in 1937, scholar Philip Sherlock (i) __________ the nationalistic feeling with which Marson's poems are imbued. Although this quality of Marson's poetry struck Sherlock as a sign of her emotional generosity (he remarked on her great "love of her homeland and its people"), Sherlock's (ii) __________ was not (iii) __________: he found some of Marson's poems excessive in their emotionality.

Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　noted; approbation; unqualified

By thus (i) __________ films, archives would not only serve as repositories but would provide (ii) __________ as well.The author suggests that cinema archives should become more like museums, justifying their existence by selecting, grouping and commenting on important films. By thus (i) __________ films, archives would not only serve as repositories but would provide (ii) __________ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2

In seventeenth-century science, we witness the first instance of technological __________: scientific tools, constructed from existing scientific knowledge, led to the acquisition of new knowledge that in turn contributed to improving the tools' design.In seventeenth-century science, we witness the first instance of technological __________: scientific tools, constructed from existing scientific knowledge, led to the acquisition of new knowledge that in turn contributed to improving the tools' design.
答案：B　feedback

答案：A, E　equivocal; ambiguous

#150 TC TRIPLE
Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific works validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific works validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed

#151 TC DOUBLE
Despite the fact that the book promises a complete rethinking of the factors contributing to the conflict, the picture that the book paints is (i) __________: in identifying cause, it is more orthodox than (ii) __________.Despite the fact that the book promises a complete rethinking of the factors contributing to the conflict, the picture that the book paints is (i) __________: in identifying cause, it is more orthodox than (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　familiar; revisionist

#152 SE
Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.
答案：D, F　prosaic; pedestrian
prosaic

#153 TC SINGLE
When it comes to cheating in competitive sports and games, chess is a particular case that might seem all but __________, since after all the board and its pieces are out in the open for all to see.When it comes to cheating in competitive sports and games, chess is a particular case that might seem all but __________, since after all the board and its pieces are out in the open for all to see.
答案：A　invulnerable
case
case: standard unit headword
#154 TC TRIPLE
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)__________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)__________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
exotic
exotic: standard unit headword
#155 TC DOUBLE
Collecting such fragments of contemporary popular culture as postcards, newspaper clippings, and wallpaper patterns, Susan Hiller transforms these seemingly (i) __________ artifacts into objective (ii) __________ by making them the centerpieces in her compositions.Collecting such fragments of contemporary popular culture as postcards, newspaper clippings, and wallpaper patterns, Susan Hiller transforms these seemingly (i) __________ artifacts into objective (ii) __________ by making them the centerpieces in her compositions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　trivial; importance

Compared to extreme weather events such as tornadoes, tropical storms, and severe floods, droughts appear almost (i) __________: they (ii) __________ of storms and floods, appearing instead as "slow-motion" disasters whose beginnings and ends are often hard to identify.Compared to extreme weather events such as tornadoes, tropical storms, and severe floods, droughts appear almost (i) __________: they (ii) __________ of storms and floods, appearing instead as "slow-motion" disasters whose beginnings and ends are often hard to identify.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　prosaic; lack the violent destructiveness

The museum exhibit perplexingly mixed significant cultural artifacts with gaudy trinkets, leading one critic to suggest that the exhibit's curator could not distinguish the important pieces from those that were __________.The museum exhibit perplexingly mixed significant cultural artifacts with gaudy trinkets, leading one critic to suggest that the exhibit's curator could not distinguish the important pieces from those that were __________.
答案：E　meretricious
artifact
artifact: standard unit headword
#158 SE
There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is __________ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is __________ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.
答案：D, F　awkward; ungainly

#159 TC TRIPLE
Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　acuity; social and cultural context; outstrip
outstrip
outstrip: standard unit headword
#160 TC SINGLE
Although preserved dinosaur tracks are themselves inherently __________, they can provide information about how the creatures moved, serving as evidence of a dynamism the tracks themselves do not possess.Although preserved dinosaur tracks are themselves inherently __________, they can provide information about how the creatures moved, serving as evidence of a dynamism the tracks themselves do not possess.
答案：C　static
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#161 SE
Some experts estimate that the recreational salmon fishery in British Columbia contributes more to the province’s economy than the commercial salmon fishery does—a surprising statistic given the political commercial __________ of the fishery in the province.Some experts estimate that the recreational salmon fishery in British Columbia contributes more to the province’s economy than the commercial salmon fishery does—a surprising statistic given the political commercial __________ of the fishery in the province.
答案：B, E　prominence; salience
contribute
contribute: standard unit headword
#162 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　mitigated; ascertaining the contents
mitigate
mitigate: standard unit headword
#163 TC TRIPLE
Unambiguous texts can allow their readers to (i)__________ them quickly, but ambiguous texts can have the attractive (ii)__________ of multiple possible interpretations, all of which can be considered equally (iii)__________, and none of which is the single true meaning.Unambiguous texts can allow their readers to (i)__________ them quickly, but ambiguous texts can have the attractive (ii)__________ of multiple possible interpretations, all of which can be considered equally (iii)__________, and none of which is the single true meaning.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　comprehend; allure; valid
ambiguous
ambiguous: standard unit headword
#164 TC SINGLE
In 2009, the author noted that the phrases "genetic therapy" and "gene therapy" had become ubiquitous and were, for that reason, __________, since the work of genetic researchers had yet to result in any actual therapeutic applications.In 2009, the author noted that the phrases "genetic therapy" and "gene therapy" had become ubiquitous and were, for that reason, __________, since the work of genetic researchers had yet to result in any actual therapeutic applications.
答案：A　misleading
note
note: standard unit headword
#165 SE
Those who read Empson’s correspondence for the first time may be disappointed that so much of it is professional rather than personal, but the distinction in this case is __________: every letter bears the full stamp of Empson’s personality.Those who read Empson’s correspondence for the first time may be disappointed that so much of it is professional rather than personal, but the distinction in this case is __________: every letter bears the full stamp of Empson’s personality.
答案：B, D　artificial; spurious
case
case: standard unit headword
#166 TC DOUBLE
The idea of traveling in order to find natural extremes and wonders was (i) __________ in eighteenth- and nineteenth-century Europe, and many European travelers of the period were drawn to Iceland and other North Atlantic countries as places where they might find these (ii) __________ landscapes.The idea of traveling in order to find natural extremes and wonders was (i) __________ in eighteenth- and nineteenth-century Europe, and many European travelers of the period were drawn to Iceland and other North Atlantic countries as places where they might find these (ii) __________ landscapes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　not uncommon; exotic
exotic
exotic: standard unit headword
#167 TC TRIPLE
The experimental theater company's members know that their performances (i)__________ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)__________ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics' derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)__________ the company's work.The experimental theater company's members know that their performances (i)__________ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)__________ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics' derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)__________ the company's work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood
experimental
experimental: standard unit headword
#168 TC SINGLE
Defenders of Szynski's work claim that he has been __________ not for his science, but merely because of his self-aggrandizing writing style.Defenders of Szynski's work claim that he has been __________ not for his science, but merely because of his self-aggrandizing writing style.
答案：A　disparaged
aggrandize
aggrandize: standard unit headword
#169 SE
The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market's sometimes destructive vicissitudes being __________ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market's sometimes destructive vicissitudes being __________ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.
答案：A, E　tempered; mitigated
mitigate
mitigate: standard unit headword
#170 TC TRIPLE
Traditional Vietnamese culture has long promoted the idea of gender equality. Founding myths (i) __________ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii) __________ actual processes. In reality, gender-based (iii) __________ persists.Traditional Vietnamese culture has long promoted the idea of gender equality. Founding myths (i) __________ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii) __________ actual processes. In reality, gender-based (iii) __________ persists.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　celebrate; incommensurate with; stratification
case
case: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　failure; indifference to
molder

#172 SE
The __________ response from her opponent indicated that the politician's comments had done little to diffuse the acrimony of their nine-month-long dispute.The __________ response from her opponent indicated that the politician's comments had done little to diffuse the acrimony of their nine-month-long dispute.
答案：B, D　scathing; vitriolic
vitriolic

#173 TC SINGLE
The law student found traditional legal reference works unhelpful, since such works assume from the reader a considerable familiarity with the intricacies of contract law, an assumption that was, in his case at least, __________.The law student found traditional legal reference works unhelpful, since such works assume from the reader a considerable familiarity with the intricacies of contract law, an assumption that was, in his case at least, __________.
答案：B　unwarranted
case
case: standard unit headword
#174 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more often considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more often considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
tilt
tilt: standard unit headword
#175 TC DOUBLE
Making loans and fighting poverty are normally two of the least glamorous pursuits around, but remarkably enough put the two together, and you have an economic innovation that has become not just (i) __________ but downright (ii) __________.Making loans and fighting poverty are normally two of the least glamorous pursuits around, but remarkably enough put the two together, and you have an economic innovation that has become not just (i) __________ but downright (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　popular; chic
downright

#176 TC DOUBLE
Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　evade; opprobrium
opprobrium

Some experts now feel that exercise should not be regarded as __________ other treatments for depression: they believe that exercise can itself serve as an appropriate remedy for some forms of the mood disorder.Some experts now feel that exercise should not be regarded as __________ other treatments for depression: they believe that exercise can itself serve as an appropriate remedy for some forms of the mood disorder.
答案：D　an adjunct to
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#178 SE
With regard to verity, neutrality, and transparency, nothing about the Internet makes it any different than Gutenberg’s printing press, which could serve __________ just as well as truth.With regard to verity, neutrality, and transparency, nothing about the Internet makes it any different than Gutenberg’s printing press, which could serve __________ just as well as truth.
答案：D, E　disinformation; mendacity
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#179 TC TRIPLE
The author of this (i)__________ memoir is notable for an unmitigated (ii)__________ that is matched only by an utter lack of self-awareness. Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.The author of this (i)__________ memoir is notable for an unmitigated (ii)__________ that is matched only by an utter lack of self-awareness. Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　self-serving; self-regard; self-aggrandizement
notableserveself-effacing
notable: standard unit headword | serve: standard unit headword | self-effacing: standard unit headword
#180 TC SINGLE
Melinda's political beliefs were a matter of __________: she held true only to those positions that served her own best interest.Melinda's political beliefs were a matter of __________: she held true only to those positions that served her own best interest.
答案：C　expediency
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#181 SE
In Ramachandran’s opinion, it is perfectly acceptable to propose bold speculations about the brain, even if these speculations seem __________; as Ramachandran frequently remarks, science thrives on risky conjecture.In Ramachandran’s opinion, it is perfectly acceptable to propose bold speculations about the brain, even if these speculations seem __________; as Ramachandran frequently remarks, science thrives on risky conjecture.
答案：A, F　unfounded; baseless
bold
bold: standard unit headword
#182 TC TRIPLE
Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)__________. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)__________ but not always lively and readable. (iii)__________, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; a staleness
lucrativeerudite
lucrative: standard unit headword | erudite: standard unit headword
#183 TC DOUBLE
It can be (i)__________ to read Margaret Fuller's travel writing, as she produced accounts of her travel that (ii)__________ conventions of bourgeois travel narrative, often capitulating to the most well-worn cliches of the genre at precisely the moments when she sought most energetically to cast them off in favor of some new, more passionate mode of discernment.It can be (i)__________ to read Margaret Fuller's travel writing, as she produced accounts of her travel that (ii)__________ conventions of bourgeois travel narrative, often capitulating to the most well-worn cliches of the genre at precisely the moments when she sought most energetically to cast them off in favor of some new, more passionate mode of discernment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　frustrating; conformed to
cast
cast: standard unit headword
#184 TC SINGLE
Before divulging the nature of their experiments, the researchers anticipated __________, thinking that their work might appear macabre; hence they prepared carefully for a press conference at which they emphasized the project's scientific and medical value.Before divulging the nature of their experiments, the researchers anticipated __________, thinking that their work might appear macabre; hence they prepared carefully for a press conference at which they emphasized the project's scientific and medical value.
答案：B　adverse repercussions

答案：A, C　wayward; errant

These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.

答案：C, F　exacerbated; aggravated

Blank 1
Blank 2

#194 TC TRIPLE
Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　limitation; decreased over time; a decoupling of
contribute
contribute: standard unit headword

Blank 2

答案：A　polemical

答案：B, D　humility; modesty

These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.

Blank 2

Reliance on precedent (i) __________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii) __________ a judge's ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.

Blank 2
答案：A, E　promotes; constrains

Politicians who invoke the founders of the United States in support of their views seem to imply that the founders consistently concurred in their own views when in reality they were a highly __________ group of thinkers.Politicians who invoke the founders of the United States in support of their views seem to imply that the founders consistently concurred in their own views when in reality they were a highly __________ group of thinkers.
答案：D　contentious

#201 SE
Because archaeology explores the most profound changes in human history by means of a grossly incomplete record, it has invited the sort of bold, imaginative interpretation in which speculation too easily becomes __________ evidence.Because archaeology explores the most profound changes in human history by means of a grossly incomplete record, it has invited the sort of bold, imaginative interpretation in which speculation too easily becomes __________ evidence.
答案：C, D　untethered from; divorced from

Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)__________ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)__________ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)__________ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)__________ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　hampered; disparate; impediment to

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：B　belied

答案：E, F　bias; orientation

Hadji Murad is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale's creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy's nonfiction.Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Hadji Murad is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale's creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy's nonfiction.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　a preference for; suppress; didacticism

#210 TC TRIPLE
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage

#211 TC DOUBLE
Not only is the field of behavioral genetics strewn with (i) __________ findings, but even among those findings that managed to survive, many have turned out to be (ii) __________ a very restricted class of cases.Not only is the field of behavioral genetics strewn with (i) __________ findings, but even among those findings that managed to survive, many have turned out to be (ii) __________ a very restricted class of cases.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　repudiated; germane to
case
case: standard unit headword
#212 TC SINGLE
Like her literary heroine, George Eliot, Barbara Kingsolvers is an old-fashioned __________, deeply curious about all branches of human learning.Like her literary heroine, George Eliot, Barbara Kingsolvers is an old-fashioned __________, deeply curious about all branches of human learning.
答案：B　polymath

philistine: standard unit headword
#213 SE
The critic claims that, contrary to its reputation for __________, the novelist's prose is full of opaque language games.The critic claims that, contrary to its reputation for __________, the novelist's prose is full of opaque language games.
答案：B, D　simplicity; artlessness
prose
prose: standard unit headword
#214 TC TRIPLE
The wealthy donor was known for his annual acts of (i) __________ throughout the community, but even more (ii) __________ was the fact that he was willing to get his hands dirty and serve the needy through hard physical labor as well as through (iii) __________ and gifts.The wealthy donor was known for his annual acts of (i) __________ throughout the community, but even more (ii) __________ was the fact that he was willing to get his hands dirty and serve the needy through hard physical labor as well as through (iii) __________ and gifts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　largesse; laudable; endowments

serve: standard unit headword

Psychologists note that in making purchase decisions, consumers often unconsciously use mental shortcuts, such as familiarity with a brand name to (i) __________ routine problems efficiently. Thus, part of what companies aim for developing a brand is very specifically to (ii) __________ consumers' conscious thought.Psychologists note that in making purchase decisions, consumers often unconsciously use mental shortcuts, such as familiarity with a brand name to (i) __________ routine problems efficiently. Thus, part of what companies aim for developing a brand is very specifically to (ii) __________ consumers' conscious thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　resolve; circumvent

note: standard unit headword
#216 TC SINGLE
The notion that prehistoric farming of Micronesia’s Mariana Islands degraded the soils upon which savannas now occur is __________ by soil scientists, who attribute nutrient-poor savanna soils instead to long geological periods of tropical weathering.The notion that prehistoric farming of Micronesia’s Mariana Islands degraded the soils upon which savannas now occur is __________ by soil scientists, who attribute nutrient-poor savanna soils instead to long geological periods of tropical weathering.
答案：C　contradicted

elucidate: standard unit headword

答案：B, C　plethora; surfeit
case
case: standard unit headword
#218 TC TRIPLE
People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)__________, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)__________ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)__________ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)__________, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)__________ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)__________ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in

divulge: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　forsaken; conventional
experimental
experimental: standard unit headword

答案：E　unexploited
deplete
deplete: standard unit headword
#221 TC TRIPLE
Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)__________ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)__________, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community's (iii)__________ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)__________ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)__________, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community's (iii)__________ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#222 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　limited; given

note: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　eradicate; extirpate

If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition, since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition, since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build population from scratch.
答案：B　augmenting

#225 TC TRIPLE
Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　utopia; prompting; the ideal location for one to better oneself
