In his study of Senegambian cleric Shaykh Mass Kah (1827-1936), Bala S. K. Saho notes that it is difficult to fully (i) __________ many of the oral accounts on which the study relies. Saho's work shows, however, that despite this absence of (ii) __________, oral history can provide useful sources from which historians can reconstruct the past.In his study of Senegambian cleric Shaykh Mass Kah (1827-1936), Bala S. K. Saho notes that it is difficult to fully (i) __________ many of the oral accounts on which the study relies. Saho's work shows, however, that despite this absence of (ii) __________, oral history can provide useful sources from which historians can reconstruct the past.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　validate; corroboration

答案：C, E　ephemeral; transitory

答案：D　maudlin

accommodate: standard unit headword
#229 TC TRIPLE
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)__________ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)__________ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)__________.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)__________ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)__________ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory evidence

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　limitations of; clear

答案：B, F　placatory; conciliatory

serve: standard unit headword

答案：C　require

However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work's validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)__________ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)__________ replication or extension may be (iii)__________ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed

答案：D, E　mendacious; deceitful

答案：B　elide

The question of who should reduce emissions is not (i) __________. Rather, according to Sinden, it is the question of who should bear the costs of these reductions that is (ii) __________.Sinden notes that in the face of warnings about the perils of climate change, the need to agree on strategies for reducing greenhouse-gas emissions is increasingly urgent. The question of who should reduce emissions is not (i) __________. Rather, according to Sinden, it is the question of who should bear the costs of these reductions that is (ii) __________.

Blank 2

答案：A　encomiums

答案：C, E　unpredictability; fickleness

Constantly referring to the bold challenges and tremendous opportunities that she and the country face, the prime minister insists that she is (iii)__________ the country, when most people would be grateful if she succeeded just in improving it a bit.The prime minister underestimates the fatigue that her continual (i)__________ induces, even among those who are largely (ii)__________ her aims. Constantly referring to the bold challenges and tremendous opportunities that she and the country face, the prime minister insists that she is (iii)__________ the country, when most people would be grateful if she succeeded just in improving it a bit.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　overclaiming; sympathetic to; radically transforming

But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. Blank 1
Blank 2

As a relative proposition, however, her position was more (ii) __________: compared to other groups in New Guinea, the Mountain Arapesh might have seemed unaggressive and unwarlike.Mead was certainly (i) __________ to state the case for Arapesh nonviolence and gentleness in absolute terms, for the Mountain Arapesh had an established tradition of warfare. Blank 2

答案：E, F　concision; pithiness

Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebos often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　get; incidental to; powerful antidote to

Blank 1
Blank 2

Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i) __________. Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility

Conservationists tend to be (i) __________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) __________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.Conservationists tend to be (i) __________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) __________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　uncomfortable with; utilitarian

答案：E, F　workaday; quotidian

#264 TC SINGLE
In the ancient world, truths about the phenomenal world were expected to be established by proof rather than by discovery because deductive logic was considered to be __________ observation and experiment.In the ancient world, truths about the phenomenal world were expected to be established by proof rather than by discovery because deductive logic was considered to be __________ observation and experiment.
答案：E　superior to

inherent: standard unit headword
#265 TC TRIPLE
The fall of the monarchical order produced a score of fragile successor states in Europe that (i)__________ ethnic discontent and revanchism. This (ii)__________, when fused with those states' inherent feebleness, makes for power voids and political (iii)__________.The fall of the monarchical order produced a score of fragile successor states in Europe that (i)__________ ethnic discontent and revanchism. This (ii)__________, when fused with those states' inherent feebleness, makes for power voids and political (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　simmered with; volatility; fluidities
inherent
inherent: standard unit headword
#266 TC DOUBLE
Considering that experimental treatments that are (i) __________ one mouse population are, nonetheless, ineffectual with other mice, we might reasonably conclude that many rodent studies are not (ii) __________.Considering that experimental treatments that are (i) __________ one mouse population are, nonetheless, ineffectual with other mice, we might reasonably conclude that many rodent studies are not (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　successful for; methodologically rigorous
experimental
experimental: standard unit headword
#267 SE
My colleagues __________ the journalist's vaunted expertise at securing high-profile interviews, declaring that, given her family's connections to celebrities, obtaining such interviews must be no more difficult than asking a friend out for coffee.My colleagues __________ the journalist's vaunted expertise at securing high-profile interviews, declaring that, given her family's connections to celebrities, obtaining such interviews must be no more difficult than asking a friend out for coffee.
答案：B, C　disparaged; dismissed
secure
secure: standard unit headword
#268 TC TRIPLE
Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　alarming; reassuring; unexceptional
speculate
speculate: standard unit headword
#269 TC DOUBLE
Boostrom argues that many scholars are too (i) __________ a tendency toward (ii) __________ in academic prose. It may even be, he says, that some academics deliberately befog their subject with in-group jargon in hopes of lending substance to their thinking.Boostrom argues that many scholars are too (i) __________ a tendency toward (ii) __________ in academic prose. It may even be, he says, that some academics deliberately befog their subject with in-group jargon in hopes of lending substance to their thinking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　forgiving of; obfuscation
prose
prose: standard unit headword
#270 SE
Critics have raved about the beauty of Peck's prose, but in his fiction, at least, the diction has only gotten more portentous with time, the writing more overwrought, and the symbolism more __________.Critics have raved about the beauty of Peck's prose, but in his fiction, at least, the diction has only gotten more portentous with time, the writing more overwrought, and the symbolism more __________.
答案：C, E　ungainly; awkward
prose
prose: standard unit headword
#271 TC TRIPLE
By the end of the 1970s, the postmodern novel had degenerated from a bold attempt to (i)__________ the conventions of traditional narrative into a literary style as (ii)__________ as any other. There are, it seems, (iii)__________ number of ways to avoid telling a straightforward story.By the end of the 1970s, the postmodern novel had degenerated from a bold attempt to (i)__________ the conventions of traditional narrative into a literary style as (ii)__________ as any other. There are, it seems, (iii)__________ number of ways to avoid telling a straightforward story.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　subvert; predictable; a limited
bold
bold: standard unit headword
#272 TC DOUBLE
The prioritization by academic institutions of scholarly publication as a criterion for advancement in science departments discourages researchers from sharing their data with peers. As a result, scientists operate in a realm where the incentive structure serves as an (i) __________ greater (ii) __________ in the profession.The prioritization by academic institutions of scholarly publication as a criterion for advancement in science departments discourages researchers from sharing their data with peers. As a result, scientists operate in a realm where the incentive structure serves as an (i) __________ greater (ii) __________ in the profession.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　impediment to; collaboration
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#273 SE
In Grey Gardens, the Maysles brothers defy the conventions of pure documentary technique by inserting themselves into the picture, __________ that serves to personalize the movie.In Grey Gardens, the Maysles brothers defy the conventions of pure documentary technique by inserting themselves into the picture, __________ that serves to personalize the movie.
答案：C, F　a transgression; a breach
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#274 TC TRIPLE
As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relativity have been astonishingly (i) __________ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii) __________ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relativity have been astonishingly (i) __________ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii) __________ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　successful; posit; deeply at odds with each other
note
note: standard unit headword
#275 TC DOUBLE
Nostalgia is to history as celebrity is to fame. Both nostalgia and celebrity are ways for the observer to (i) __________ things that are exotic, and that are characterized by their (ii) __________ the ordinary, and to make them, instead, familiar matters upon which the observer's own feelings can feed.Nostalgia is to history as celebrity is to fame. Both nostalgia and celebrity are ways for the observer to (i) __________ things that are exotic, and that are characterized by their (ii) __________ the ordinary, and to make them, instead, familiar matters upon which the observer's own feelings can feed.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　appropriate; difference from
exotic
exotic: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　cursory; perfunctory

serve: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　concision; sprawling; economical

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　in overall equilibrium; fluctuations

答案：B, E　virtuosity; mastery

#280 TC TRIPLE
Some scientists speculate that changes in atmospheric methane were responsible for some abrupt global warming events in the past such as the one at the end of the geologic period known as the Younger Dryas. There is good evidence that large quantities of methane have been released quickly in the past through natural processes; however, methane is a relatively (i)__________ molecule in the atmosphere and thus (ii)__________ through chemical reactions with other compounds. The effect of even a large increase would therefore be (iii)__________.Some scientists speculate that changes in atmospheric methane were responsible for some abrupt global warming events in the past such as the one at the end of the geologic period known as the Younger Dryas. There is good evidence that large quantities of methane have been released quickly in the past through natural processes; however, methane is a relatively (i)__________ molecule in the atmosphere and thus (ii)__________ through chemical reactions with other compounds. The effect of even a large increase would therefore be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unstable; fairly quickly destroyed; ephemeral

speculate: standard unit headword
#281 TC DOUBLE
It is often the case that a certain intellectual advantage may be found by being (i) __________ an established field of knowledge, but even if you accept the argument about the importance of (ii) __________ thinking, it is hardly the only significant thinking there is.It is often the case that a certain intellectual advantage may be found by being (i) __________ an established field of knowledge, but even if you accept the argument about the importance of (ii) __________ thinking, it is hardly the only significant thinking there is.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　on the periphery of; outsider

答案：D, E　belie; mask

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　documents; imagination; fanciful

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　duplicity; dissemblance
in spite of
in spite of: standard unit headword
#285 SE
Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ __________ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ __________ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.
答案：A, C　somnolent; lethargic
befuddle
befuddle: standard unit headword
#286 TC TRIPLE
If companies were taxed based on the amount of their atmospheric emissions of pollutants, they could make their own decisions about how to alter their operations. Firms could compare the cost of paying taxes with the cost of preventing the emissions in the first place and would, in most cases, do a bit of both. Firms that can reduce pollution (i)__________ would reduce it quite (ii)__________, while those that can only do so at great cost would (iii)__________.If companies were taxed based on the amount of their atmospheric emissions of pollutants, they could make their own decisions about how to alter their operations. Firms could compare the cost of paying taxes with the cost of preventing the emissions in the first place and would, in most cases, do a bit of both. Firms that can reduce pollution (i)__________ would reduce it quite (ii)__________, while those that can only do so at great cost would (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　cheaply; substantially; curtail less
case
case: standard unit headword

At its best, the storytelling in this television series manages to accommodate a sense of historical (i) __________. The narrative shows that American independence was not (ii) __________. It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.At its best, the storytelling in this television series manages to accommodate a sense of historical (i) __________. The narrative shows that American independence was not (ii) __________. It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　contingency; inevitable

Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.
答案：A, B　manifest; reveal

#289 TC TRIPLE
Materials expand and contract with temperature changes. This general concept has been exploited for thousands of years, for instance, to ensure tight rivets in blacksmithing by shrink-fitting. For precision instruments, however, (i)__________ materials are truly (ii)__________: hence the importance of Invar, an alloy notable for its (iii)__________ expansion and contraction over a range of temperatures.Materials expand and contract with temperature changes. This general concept has been exploited for thousands of years, for instance, to ensure tight rivets in blacksmithing by shrink-fitting. For precision instruments, however, (i)__________ materials are truly (ii)__________: hence the importance of Invar, an alloy notable for its (iii)__________ expansion and contraction over a range of temperatures.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　dimensionally stable; paramount; resistance to

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　reject; entrenched

答案：A, C　unconventional; quirky

It provides second chances, in other words. Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　antidote; confusion; riddled with errors

High self-esteem has recently assumed a somewhat (i) __________ status in social psychology. On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.High self-esteem has recently assumed a somewhat (i) __________ status in social psychology. On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：C, D　paradoxical; antisocial

答案：A, B　beneficence; altruism

They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) __________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) __________.The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) __________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) __________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　prosper; benefits from; perpetuated

As a result, her inventive dishes (ii) __________ the palate.

Blank 2
答案：B, F　hidebound by; delight

答案：C, D　brook; tolerate

Unlike news reports, theater isn't expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i) __________, the form, by nature, traffics in (ii) __________. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i) __________, the form, by nature, traffics in (ii) __________. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what's real and what's (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　quality; remained constant

答案：C, E　spirited; vigorous

Unlike those glossy Architectural Digest photographs that are deliberately (i) __________ lest they distract from a focus on the building itself, Friedlander's architectural photography is the product of his insistence that he is an individual looking at buildings and scenes as they actually exist. Friedlander reminds us that everyday objects (ii) __________ or, more exactly, that the unimpeded visual scene of a building without cars or power lines or street signs is a highly (iii) __________ photographic artifact.Unlike those glossy Architectural Digest photographs that are deliberately (i) __________ lest they distract from a focus on the building itself, Friedlander's architectural photography is the product of his insistence that he is an individual looking at buildings and scenes as they actually exist. Friedlander reminds us that everyday objects (ii) __________ or, more exactly, that the unimpeded visual scene of a building without cars or power lines or street signs is a highly (iii) __________ photographic artifact.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　impersonal; normally interfere; contrived

The Newseum is a museum created by, for, and about journalists and newshounds. But for all its celebration of the news industry, its creators have taken care to avoid (i) __________. Along with the many testimonials to journalistic courage and a memorial to journalists who lost their lives on the job, the museum presents noteworthy examples of journalistic (ii) __________.The Newseum is a museum created by, for, and about journalists and newshounds. But for all its celebration of the news industry, its creators have taken care to avoid (i) __________. Along with the many testimonials to journalistic courage and a memorial to journalists who lost their lives on the job, the museum presents noteworthy examples of journalistic (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　puffery; malfeasance

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derision; appositeness; willingness to flatter

Blank 1
Blank 2

The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do __________ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do __________ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.
答案：C, E　succumb to; yield to

#310 TC TRIPLE
Since readily available computer programs now allow photographers to manipulate their images with the same (i) __________ as painters, photography's claim to any inherent truth has just about evaporated. But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.Since readily available computer programs now allow photographers to manipulate their images with the same (i) __________ as painters, photography's claim to any inherent truth has just about evaporated. But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　license for fiction; fictive; integral
inherent
inherent: standard unit headword
#311 TC DOUBLE
The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)__________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)__________.The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)__________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　narrow in scope; parochialism
susceptible
susceptible: standard unit headword
#312 SE
Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to __________ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to __________ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.
答案：A, E　hamstring; impair
reinvent
reinvent: standard unit headword
#313 TC TRIPLE
A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　speculative; keep it up; gratuitous
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#314 TC DOUBLE
The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)__________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)__________.The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)__________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　narrow in scope; parochialism
susceptible
susceptible: standard unit headword
#315 SE
Creativity is no longer seen as __________ inspiration leading to a poem or a painting; it has come to be thought of as something permeating the whole of a person’s life.Creativity is no longer seen as __________ inspiration leading to a poem or a painting; it has come to be thought of as something permeating the whole of a person’s life.
答案：B, D　a momentary; an evanescent
prosaic
prosaic: standard unit headword
#316 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#317 TC DOUBLE
The author of this travel guide (i)__________ to show readers Cairo as it really is, but his information is not reliable. For instance, his geography is (ii)__________, with one walking tour covering areas of the city that are twenty miles apart.The author of this travel guide (i)__________ to show readers Cairo as it really is, but his information is not reliable. For instance, his geography is (ii)__________, with one walking tour covering areas of the city that are twenty miles apart.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　purports; erratic
erudite
erudite: standard unit headword
#318 SE
The nation's robust economic performance could be __________ by the persistent flaws in its economic institutions and structure that are the result of half-finished and misguided government policies.The nation's robust economic performance could be __________ by the persistent flaws in its economic institutions and structure that are the result of half-finished and misguided government policies.
答案：C, D　undermined; impaired
outstrip
outstrip: standard unit headword
#319 TC TRIPLE
Because popular culture often portrays lawyers as shady and scientists as eccentric, we might expect cinematic depictions of scientists employed as expert witnesses in court cases to reflect badly on both law and science. Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.Because popular culture often portrays lawyers as shady and scientists as eccentric, we might expect cinematic depictions of scientists employed as expert witnesses in court cases to reflect badly on both law and science. Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　suffer reputational losses; tainted; a counterweight to
case
case: standard unit headword
#320 TC DOUBLE
People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentments if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentments if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　clandestine; open
equitable
equitable: standard unit headword
#321 SE
Today the chair of task force in charge of revising the psychiatric diagnostic manual is __________ post—people work for years to position themselves as candidates—but in the early 1970s, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.Today the chair of task force in charge of revising the psychiatric diagnostic manual is __________ post—people work for years to position themselves as candidates—but in the early 1970s, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.
答案：A, E　a coveted; an enviable
lucrative
lucrative: standard unit headword
#322 TC TRIPLE
Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　deflects; untrue; gauge its veracity
note
note: standard unit headword
#323 TC DOUBLE
People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentment if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentment if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　clandestine; open
equitable
equitable: standard unit headword
#324 SE
In Schaller’s contradictory introduction to the book, she alternately applauds and __________ humankind’s role in animal conservation.In Schaller’s contradictory introduction to the book, she alternately applauds and __________ humankind’s role in animal conservation.
答案：C, F　denounces; lambastes
disclose
disclose: standard unit headword
#325 TC TRIPLE
Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　superior to; closer to; apologize for
epic
epic: standard unit headword
#326 TC DOUBLE
Many scholars have argued that the United States Supreme Court usually (i)__________ public opinion in its decisions because it fears that it will (ii)__________ if it does not; when it does depart from public opinion, it whips up political maelstrom.Many scholars have argued that the United States Supreme Court usually (i)__________ public opinion in its decisions because it fears that it will (ii)__________ if it does not; when it does depart from public opinion, it whips up political maelstrom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　hews to; lose public support
mitigate
mitigate: standard unit headword
#327 SE
Readers have long considered Lawd Today, Richard Wright's first written and last published novel, __________; the novel simply seems inconsistent with the image of what a Wright text should do or be.Readers have long considered Lawd Today, Richard Wright's first written and last published novel, __________; the novel simply seems inconsistent with the image of what a Wright text should do or be.
答案：C, E　an anomaly; an aberration
artifact
artifact: standard unit headword
#328 TC TRIPLE
It has been suggested that the use of metaphors from the realms of battle and sports to describe arguments contributes to the (i) __________ of the argumentative process. Such metaphors may (ii) __________ adversarial conceptions of argument, leading interlocutors to behave aggressively in verbal disputes, thereby (iii) __________ a process that ideally leads to resolution.It has been suggested that the use of metaphors from the realms of battle and sports to describe arguments contributes to the (i) __________ of the argumentative process. Such metaphors may (ii) __________ adversarial conceptions of argument, leading interlocutors to behave aggressively in verbal disputes, thereby (iii) __________ a process that ideally leads to resolution.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　breakdown; promote; undermining
contribute
contribute: standard unit headword
#329 TC DOUBLE
The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)__________ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) __________ as the articles themselves.The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)__________ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) __________ as the articles themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　unrealistic; informative
juvenile
juvenile: standard unit headword
#330 SE
As a historical genre, biography is best when __________, a careful reconstruction of the past in all its unfamiliar particularity.As a historical genre, biography is best when __________, a careful reconstruction of the past in all its unfamiliar particularity.
答案：E, F　meticulous; thorough
concise
concise: standard unit headword
#331 TC TRIPLE
Unlike some of his predecessors and competitors, Schaeffer's distinguished position as a foreign-affairs columnist has not made him (i) __________. His column is breezy and conversational. In light-handed prose he reports what he sees with his own eyes and what he learns from his trips around the globe. To further (ii) __________ an already (iii) __________ style, he shares with his readers occasional heartwarming references to his family.Unlike some of his predecessors and competitors, Schaeffer's distinguished position as a foreign-affairs columnist has not made him (i) __________. His column is breezy and conversational. In light-handed prose he reports what he sees with his own eyes and what he learns from his trips around the globe. To further (ii) __________ an already (iii) __________ style, he shares with his readers occasional heartwarming references to his family.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　pontifical; enliven; familiar
prose
prose: standard unit headword
#332 TC DOUBLE
As late as the 1990s, Merry Wiesner Hanks argued that developments during the Reformation and the Enlightenment had (i)__________ effects on the participation of women in the field of medicine. She claimed that the (ii)__________ Reformation and Enlightenment women left female health practitioners restricted to assisting women and children in an unpaid and unrecognized capacity.As late as the 1990s, Merry Wiesner Hanks argued that developments during the Reformation and the Enlightenment had (i)__________ effects on the participation of women in the field of medicine. She claimed that the (ii)__________ Reformation and Enlightenment women left female health practitioners restricted to assisting women and children in an unpaid and unrecognized capacity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　dire; worsening positions of
ambiguous
ambiguous: standard unit headword
#333 SE
The territory’s tradition of simple and low taxes, combined with a comparatively easygoing government, has earned it the __________ of its citizens and is widely seen as a main reason for its stunning rise to prosperity.The territory’s tradition of simple and low taxes, combined with a comparatively easygoing government, has earned it the __________ of its citizens and is widely seen as a main reason for its stunning rise to prosperity.
答案：E, F　approbation; adulation
opprobrium
opprobrium: standard unit headword
#334 TC TRIPLE
This is not the first time science has been invoked to argue for inherent limits to the capabilities of technology. The most (i) __________ of such predictions made in the name of science, such as that we would never go to the Moon, have underestimated the (ii) __________ of engineers. But what these blunders had in common was that no law of physics, or at least no (iii) __________ one, was invoked to support the claim.This is not the first time science has been invoked to argue for inherent limits to the capabilities of technology. The most (i) __________ of such predictions made in the name of science, such as that we would never go to the Moon, have underestimated the (ii) __________ of engineers. But what these blunders had in common was that no law of physics, or at least no (iii) __________ one, was invoked to support the claim.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　embarrassing; ingenuity; established
inherent
inherent: standard unit headword
#335 TC DOUBLE
Despite disputes between the sisters lasted all summer; Megan remained (i)__________ and Lauren was equally (ii)__________.Despite disputes between the sisters lasted all summer; Megan remained (i)__________ and Lauren was equally (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　intransigent; uncompromising
feckless
feckless: standard unit headword
#336 SE
Aerial viewings of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechuan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appears crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.Aerial viewings of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechuan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appears crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.
答案：A, C　reveal; manifest
mitigate
mitigate: standard unit headword
#337 TC TRIPLE
Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain
elucidatemitigate
elucidate: standard unit headword | mitigate: standard unit headword
#338 TC DOUBLE
As he has matured as a scholar, Felmar has come to see the merit of qualification. His conclusions, which early in his career he (i)__________, are now often (ii)__________.As he has matured as a scholar, Felmar has come to see the merit of qualification. His conclusions, which early in his career he (i)__________, are now often (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　stated as absolute; hedged
reiterate
reiterate: standard unit headword
#339 SE
One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely __________ fossils throughout his career.One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely __________ fossils throughout his career.
答案：A, F　counterfeit; spurious
feckless
feckless: standard unit headword
#340 TC TRIPLE
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforces
ambiguoushearten
ambiguous: standard unit headword | hearten: standard unit headword

Except for a few passages in the biography in which the subject's flaws are (i)__________, the author's treatment of her subject is (ii)__________.Except for a few passages in the biography in which the subject's flaws are (i)__________, the author's treatment of her subject is (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　too harshly judged; judicious

erudite: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　unscrupulous; dishonest

The field of urban ecology is in __________ phase: just a decade or two ago the idea of doing natural science in a city was something few researchers ever considered.The field of urban ecology is in __________ phase: just a decade or two ago the idea of doing natural science in a city was something few researchers ever considered.
答案：B, C　an incipient; a nascent

ambiguous: standard unit headword
#346 TC TRIPLE
The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

traumatic: standard unit headword | riveting: standard unit headword

Although the dispute between the sisters lasted all summer, Megan remained (i) __________ and Lauren was equally (ii) __________.Although the dispute between the sisters lasted all summer, Megan remained (i) __________ and Lauren was equally (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　intransigent; uncompromising

Software makers typically write licensing agreements disclaiming responsibility for any negative consequences of using their products, but as computers become integrated into everything from cars to medical devices, that stance will become __________.Software makers typically write licensing agreements disclaiming responsibility for any negative consequences of using their products, but as computers become integrated into everything from cars to medical devices, that stance will become __________.
答案：B, D　untenable; indefensible

ambiguous: standard unit headword
#349 TC TRIPLE
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
contribute
contribute: standard unit headword
#350 TC DOUBLE
The author argued that the field of sociology has been overly (i) __________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii) __________.The author argued that the field of sociology has been overly (i) __________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　narrow in scope; parochialism

This is a __________ book: it takes up and defends a position to which the current intellectual climate is generally hostile.This is a __________ book: it takes up and defends a position to which the current intellectual climate is generally hostile.
答案：B, E　plucky; courageous
noteworthy
noteworthy: standard unit headword
#352 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating
aggrandize
aggrandize: standard unit headword
#353 TC DOUBLE
The author of this travel guide (i) __________ to show his readers Cairo as it really is, but his information is not reliable: for example, his geography is (ii) __________, with one walking tour covering areas of the city that are twenty miles apart.The author of this travel guide (i) __________ to show his readers Cairo as it really is, but his information is not reliable: for example, his geography is (ii) __________, with one walking tour covering areas of the city that are twenty miles apart.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　purports; erratic

#354 SE
Some consider the theorem Emmy Noether first published in 1918 to be as important as Einstein's theory of relativity, since much of today's vanguard research in physics is __________ Noether's theorem.Some consider the theorem Emmy Noether first published in 1918 to be as important as Einstein's theory of relativity, since much of today's vanguard research in physics is __________ Noether's theorem.
答案：B, D　undergirded by; dependent on
elucidate
elucidate: standard unit headword
#355 TC TRIPLE
Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from
mercenary
mercenary: standard unit headword

The author paints a rather dark picture of book publishing as a hidebound industry, one that is facing a profound change in its mode of production but is so (i) __________ its past as to be (ii) __________ opportunity offered by technological changes.The author paints a rather dark picture of book publishing as a hidebound industry, one that is facing a profound change in its mode of production but is so (i) __________ its past as to be (ii) __________ opportunity offered by technological changes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　encumbered by; unable to seize
embolden
embolden: standard unit headword
#357 SE
Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.
答案：B, D　prescribe; codify
disclose

#358 TC TRIPLE
His idea to make money by building a drone that could fly from the West Coast to Hawaii while continuously sending back low-altitude weather data is a characteristically (i)__________ project. The (ii)__________ of good storm-intensity forecasts has huge economic costs, but filling that need won’t automatically generate big profits because the economic benefits are (iii)__________.His idea to make money by building a drone that could fly from the West Coast to Hawaii while continuously sending back low-altitude weather data is a characteristically (i)__________ project. The (ii)__________ of good storm-intensity forecasts has huge economic costs, but filling that need won’t automatically generate big profits because the economic benefits are (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　quixotic; dearth; so widely diffused

mercenary: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　hews to; lose public support

mitigate: standard unit headword
#360 SE
Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.
答案：C, E　a derisive; a contemptuous
ponderous
ponderous: standard unit headword
#361 TC TRIPLE
The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes
stifle
stifle: standard unit headword
#362 TC DOUBLE
The author's best-selling book on Virginia Woolf is not (i) __________ treatment of her subject; on the contrary, it presents (ii) __________ portrait of the novelist, faults and all.The author's best-selling book on Virginia Woolf is not (i) __________ treatment of her subject; on the contrary, it presents (ii) __________ portrait of the novelist, faults and all.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　an idealized; an unflinching
erudite
erudite: standard unit headword
#363 SE
Edric's imagination has always been drawn to the peripheral, to characters who are set apart or seeking a geography to match their sense of spiritual __________.Edric's imagination has always been drawn to the peripheral, to characters who are set apart or seeking a geography to match their sense of spiritual __________.
答案：A, B　exile; separation

renewal: standard unit headword
#364 TC TRIPLE
Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned
exotic
exotic: standard unit headword
#365 TC DOUBLE
As he has matured as a scholar, Felmar has come to see the merit of qualification. His conclusions, which early in his career he (i) __________, are now often (ii) __________.As he has matured as a scholar, Felmar has come to see the merit of qualification. His conclusions, which early in his career he (i) __________, are now often (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　stated as absolutes; hedged
reiterate
reiterate: standard unit headword
#366 SE
A new study may explain people's tendency to be __________ about their own futures: people asked to imagine themselves in both positive and negative future scenarios were subsequently much better at recalling the happy scenarios.A new study may explain people's tendency to be __________ about their own futures: people asked to imagine themselves in both positive and negative future scenarios were subsequently much better at recalling the happy scenarios.
答案：B, D　blithe; optimistic
befuddle
befuddle: standard unit headword

The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science--even "pure" science--can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be: scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science--even "pure" science--can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be: scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.

Blank 2

答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes

#368 TC DOUBLE
There are something like 1,400 seed banks around the world, which guard samples of crop plants ranging from alfalfa to yams. But this agricultural archive is (i) __________ as a result of war, storms, scant money, and bad management, particularly in the world's most (ii) __________ places.There are something like 1,400 seed banks around the world, which guard samples of crop plants ranging from alfalfa to yams. But this agricultural archive is (i) __________ as a result of war, storms, scant money, and bad management, particularly in the world's most (ii) __________ places.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　eroding; turbulent
secure
secure: standard unit headword
#369 SE
The museum's display of bandolier bags made by Ojibwa artists of the Mille Lacs community reinforces the idea that this community's culture is __________ one: early twentieth-century bandolier bags are displayed alongside others made recently.The museum's display of bandolier bags made by Ojibwa artists of the Mille Lacs community reinforces the idea that this community's culture is __________ one: early twentieth-century bandolier bags are displayed alongside others made recently.
答案：C, D　an unfolding; a living

inventive: standard unit headword
#370 TC TRIPLE
Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)__________ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)__________. People reasonably (iii)__________ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)__________ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)__________. People reasonably (iii)__________ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　surfeit; virtue; forgo
conundrum
conundrum: standard unit headword
#371 TC DOUBLE
Some people (i) __________ translations of great literary works--especially those insistent on a literal translation for whom no rendering is ever (ii) __________ enough.Some people (i) __________ translations of great literary works--especially those insistent on a literal translation for whom no rendering is ever (ii) __________ enough.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　dismiss; faithful
esteem
esteem: standard unit headword
#372 SE
Yoshiko Yamaguchi was a remarkably __________ public figure: she was not only an actress but also a journalist, a prominent politician in the Japanese parliament, and the wife of a diplomat.Yoshiko Yamaguchi was a remarkably __________ public figure: she was not only an actress but also a journalist, a prominent politician in the Japanese parliament, and the wife of a diplomat.
答案：A, E　protean; versatile
prosaic
prosaic: standard unit headword
#373 TC TRIPLE
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
contribute
contribute: standard unit headword
#374 TC DOUBLE
Boreal forest is at the southern boundary of the moss-dominated tundra, which remains characteristically treeless because its spongy surface retains water that cannot drain away through the underlying permafrost. But as temperatures rise the permafrost recedes, (i) __________ the (ii) __________ of forest.Boreal forest is at the southern boundary of the moss-dominated tundra, which remains characteristically treeless because its spongy surface retains water that cannot drain away through the underlying permafrost. But as temperatures rise the permafrost recedes, (i) __________ the (ii) __________ of forest.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　facilitating; incursion

renewal: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　trivia; ephemera

conundrum: standard unit headword

Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ conference, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ conference, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from

As late as the 1990s, Merry Wiesner Hanks argued that developments during the Reformation and the Enlightenment had (i) __________ effects on the participation of women in the field of medicine. She claimed that the (ii) __________ Reformation and Enlightenment women left female health practitioners restricted to assisting in an unpaid and unrecognized capacity.As late as the 1990s, Merry Wiesner Hanks argued that developments during the Reformation and the Enlightenment had (i) __________ effects on the participation of women in the field of medicine. She claimed that the (ii) __________ Reformation and Enlightenment women left female health practitioners restricted to assisting in an unpaid and unrecognized capacity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　dire; worsening positions of
ambiguous
ambiguous: standard unit headword
#378 SE
Believing fervently that very few truly good poems are written without a great deal of deliberation and revision, the poet urged her creative-writing students to be extremely __________.Believing fervently that very few truly good poems are written without a great deal of deliberation and revision, the poet urged her creative-writing students to be extremely __________.
答案：B, D　fastidious; finicky

inventive: standard unit headword
#379 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating
aggrandize
aggrandize: standard unit headword
#380 TC DOUBLE
To function as (i) __________, literary critics must write well. A badly written book review is worse than a badly written political speech or greeting card; a badly written review is (ii) __________, like a barber with a terrible haircut. If critics cannot write well, how can they authoritatively recognize and promote good writing?To function as (i) __________, literary critics must write well. A badly written book review is worse than a badly written political speech or greeting card; a badly written review is (ii) __________, like a barber with a terrible haircut. If critics cannot write well, how can they authoritatively recognize and promote good writing?
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　evangelists; self-defeating
aggrandize
aggrandize: standard unit headword
#381 SE
Given that Mr. Harris was obviously sophisticated and worldly, his failure to do well in the position can hardly be ascribed to __________.Given that Mr. Harris was obviously sophisticated and worldly, his failure to do well in the position can hardly be ascribed to __________.
答案：B, D　callowness; naivete
sloth
sloth: standard unit headword

What she disliked in the fictions of some of her peers was the excessive (i)__________. In her novel, by contrast, she was marked by a (ii)__________: she always (iii)__________.What she disliked in the fictions of some of her peers was the excessive (i)__________. In her novel, by contrast, she was marked by a (ii)__________: she always (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　explicitness; reticence; keeps some details undisclosed

Though the play crackles with humor, the dialogue is less (i) __________ when it comes to the drama's emotional core. There the players tend to spell out their emotions in (ii) __________ aphorisms, and repeat them as necessary.Though the play crackles with humor, the dialogue is less (i) __________ when it comes to the drama's emotional core. There the players tend to spell out their emotions in (ii) __________ aphorisms, and repeat them as necessary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　adroit; jejune

ambiguous: standard unit headword

Although the philosopher frequently dealt with __________ theoretical topics, she was capable of writing in a style that was comprehensible to a general audience.Although the philosopher frequently dealt with __________ theoretical topics, she was capable of writing in a style that was comprehensible to a general audience.
答案：B, E　abstruse; recondite

ponderous: standard unit headword
#385 TC TRIPLE
Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i) __________. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii) __________: our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii) __________ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i) __________. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii) __________: our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii) __________ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay

renewal: standard unit headword
#386 TC DOUBLE
Because scientific breakthroughs tend to occur when a prevailing theory's predictions start to fail, scientists sometimes find it (i) __________ when a theory persists--as the standard model of particle physics has done--in making (ii) __________ predictions.Because scientific breakthroughs tend to occur when a prevailing theory's predictions start to fail, scientists sometimes find it (i) __________ when a theory persists--as the standard model of particle physics has done--in making (ii) __________ predictions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　frustrating; correct

答案：D, F　misrepresent; underestimate

#388 TC TRIPLE
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent
susceptible
susceptible: standard unit headword

Historically, Hispanics have been (ii) __________ in the self-employment sector, so this change reduced the amount by which the Hispanic self-employment rate trailed the overall self-employment rate.During the decade 2000-2010 in the United States, the increase in self-employed Hispanic entrepreneurs (i) __________ the increase in Hispanic workers overall. Historically, Hispanics have been (ii) __________ in the self-employment sector, so this change reduced the amount by which the Hispanic self-employment rate trailed the overall self-employment rate.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　exceeded; underrepresented
underappreciated
underappreciated: standard unit headword
#390 SE
People’s decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal consideration and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often __________: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.People’s decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal consideration and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often __________: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.
答案：B, F　flummoxed; confounded

Some climatologists dismiss as (i)__________ the debate among geophysicists over the role of carbon dioxide in global climate change across many millions of years. These climatologists say the evidence of a tie between carbon dioxide and planetary warming over the last few centuries is so (ii)__________ that any longer-term evidence against such a link must somehow be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　irrelevant; compelling; tainted
