The songwriter's (i) __________ nature can lead her audiences to forget that her songs are about painful experiences just as often as they are about (ii) __________ ones.The songwriter's (i) __________ nature can lead her audiences to forget that her songs are about painful experiences just as often as they are about (ii) __________ ones.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　jocose; exultant

The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)__________ is the cowbirds' habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)__________ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)__________, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)__________ is the cowbirds' habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)__________ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)__________, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amusing; unwitting; grow quickly

#394 TC DOUBLE
Scientists were jubilant to discover a remarkably well-preserved sixteenth-century vessel in the icy waters of the Baltic Sea, but they were (i) __________ by the discovery, a lack of oxygen in icy depths can (ii) __________ the usual riot of creatures that like to feast on lost wooden ships.Scientists were jubilant to discover a remarkably well-preserved sixteenth-century vessel in the icy waters of the Baltic Sea, but they were (i) __________ by the discovery, a lack of oxygen in icy depths can (ii) __________ the usual riot of creatures that like to feast on lost wooden ships.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　not entirely surprised; discourage

Blank 1

Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　tendentious; selective; omitting

The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2

Toxicity is an (i) __________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii) __________ public health.

Blank 2

The danger often facing authors of satirical works is that if the audience is not (i) __________ the joke, the piece may end up (ii) __________ the behavior it was trying to (iii) __________.The danger often facing authors of satirical works is that if the audience is not (i) __________ the joke, the piece may end up (ii) __________ the behavior it was trying to (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　discerning enough to get; reinforcing; ridicule

The political theorist argues that passing laws to (i) __________ the state in order to preclude abuse of its citizenry sounds appealing and even necessary, but doing so can (ii) __________ the state's ability to protect its citizens from both external and internal aggression. Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.The political theorist argues that passing laws to (i) __________ the state in order to preclude abuse of its citizenry sounds appealing and even necessary, but doing so can (ii) __________ the state's ability to protect its citizens from both external and internal aggression. Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　constrain; vitiate
aggrandize
aggrandize: standard unit headword
#401 TC TRIPLE
Arguments based on mere possibilities (i) __________ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii) __________ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as "Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about" can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii) __________ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.Arguments based on mere possibilities (i) __________ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii) __________ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as "Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about" can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii) __________ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　epitomize; polarize; decisive

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　belied; intensity on

#403 TC TRIPLE
Although the Vibrio fischeri bacterium has the capacity to make all the complex nutrients it needs from a single source of carbon and nitrogen, scientists believe that if V. fischeri is in an environment that (i) __________ particular nutrients, it will use the nutrients in the environment first. This bacterium, in other words, prefers (ii) __________ to making its own meals from scratch, the (iii) __________ approach being more energy efficient and allowing the bacterium to grow much faster.Although the Vibrio fischeri bacterium has the capacity to make all the complex nutrients it needs from a single source of carbon and nitrogen, scientists believe that if V. fischeri is in an environment that (i) __________ particular nutrients, it will use the nutrients in the environment first. This bacterium, in other words, prefers (ii) __________ to making its own meals from scratch, the (iii) __________ approach being more energy efficient and allowing the bacterium to grow much faster.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　affords; getting take-out food; former
deplete
deplete: standard unit headword
#404 TC DOUBLE
Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i) __________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii) __________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i) __________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii) __________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　didactic; amount to
ambiguous
ambiguous: standard unit headword
#405 TC TRIPLE
Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i) __________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii) __________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii) __________ it.Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i) __________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii) __________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii) __________ it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　contemporaneity; compelling; embrace
ambiguous
ambiguous: standard unit headword
#406 TC DOUBLE
The lecturer (i) __________ to provide innovative insights into important issues, but her promise of originality was more than she delivered: most of her pronouncements were (ii) __________.The lecturer (i) __________ to provide innovative insights into important issues, but her promise of originality was more than she delivered: most of her pronouncements were (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undertook; platitudinous
inventive
inventive: standard unit headword
#407 TC TRIPLE
Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) __________; they were widely disparaged for being so (ii) __________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) __________.Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) __________; they were widely disparaged for being so (ii) __________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　puerile; lowbrow; abashed
hearten
hearten: standard unit headword
#408 TC DOUBLE
With its immense biomass of top predators, the Ross Sea is (i) __________ compared to other modern ocean ecosystems. Its high proportion of top predators (ii) __________ the standard textbook description of an ecological pyramid, in which primary producers-phytoplankton in ocean ecosystems-drive the system and make up most of the biomass.With its immense biomass of top predators, the Ross Sea is (i) __________ compared to other modern ocean ecosystems. Its high proportion of top predators (ii) __________ the standard textbook description of an ecological pyramid, in which primary producers-phytoplankton in ocean ecosystems-drive the system and make up most of the biomass.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　anomalous; inverts
elucidate
elucidate: standard unit headword
#409 TC TRIPLE
The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring
experimental
experimental: standard unit headword
#410 TC DOUBLE
Although fears of a dark, robot-controlled future are evident in the popular imagination, some scientists are more (i) __________ about the impact that artificial intelligence will have in real life. They share a generally (ii) __________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.Although fears of a dark, robot-controlled future are evident in the popular imagination, some scientists are more (i) __________ about the impact that artificial intelligence will have in real life. They share a generally (ii) __________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sanguine; propitious
prosaic
prosaic: standard unit headword
#411 TC TRIPLE
Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
reiterate
reiterate: standard unit headword
#412 TC DOUBLE
One school of thought regards job avoidance--missing work or arriving late--as (i) __________ leaving an unsatisfactory job. According to this view, job avoidance helps unhappy employees adjust to job frustrations and makes them less likely to quit. A competing school of thought suggests that job avoidance may simply (ii) __________ propensities to leave.One school of thought regards job avoidance--missing work or arriving late--as (i) __________ leaving an unsatisfactory job. According to this view, job avoidance helps unhappy employees adjust to job frustrations and makes them less likely to quit. A competing school of thought suggests that job avoidance may simply (ii) __________ propensities to leave.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　an alternative to; reinforce
counteract
counteract: standard unit headword
#413 TC TRIPLE
Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　difficulty of; still more acute; specious
ambiguous
ambiguous: standard unit headword
#414 TC DOUBLE
The writer, who (i) __________ commercial literary success, nevertheless (and to her detriment) (ii) __________ the intelligibility expected by most readers.The writer, who (i) __________ commercial literary success, nevertheless (and to her detriment) (ii) __________ the intelligibility expected by most readers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　longed for; shunned
contribute
contribute: standard unit headword
#415 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
ambiguous
ambiguous: standard unit headword
#416 TC DOUBLE
It is an old vice of criticism--and lately of scholarship--to assume that the meaning of artworks is (i) __________ by their creators and is then simply "there," waiting only to be decoded by a specially gifted interpreter. That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.It is an old vice of criticism--and lately of scholarship--to assume that the meaning of artworks is (i) __________ by their creators and is then simply "there," waiting only to be decoded by a specially gifted interpreter. That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　fully vested in them; vitiated
mitigate
mitigate: standard unit headword
#417 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
bold
bold: standard unit headword
#418 TC DOUBLE
Because of her founding of the Pastel Society of America and numerous other activities on behalf of pastel painting, Flora B. Giffuni is recognized as a (i) __________ of the medium. Her efforts (ii) __________ her hope that the art will reach a higher level of recognition.Because of her founding of the Pastel Society of America and numerous other activities on behalf of pastel painting, Flora B. Giffuni is recognized as a (i) __________ of the medium. Her efforts (ii) __________ her hope that the art will reach a higher level of recognition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　mainstay; reflect
mitigate
mitigate: standard unit headword
#419 TC TRIPLE
The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　inherit; tend to embody; resist
reinvent
reinvent: standard unit headword
#420 TC DOUBLE
Any account of experimental music in the United States that (i)__________ the predominantly African American bebop and free jazz movements is (ii)__________, since this body of music constitutes what is arguably the most influential African American experimental music in the decades following the Second World War.Any account of experimental music in the United States that (i)__________ the predominantly African American bebop and free jazz movements is (ii)__________, since this body of music constitutes what is arguably the most influential African American experimental music in the decades following the Second World War.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　neglects; problematic
underappreciatedserveexperimental
underappreciated: standard unit headword | serve: standard unit headword | experimental: standard unit headword
#421 TC TRIPLE
A few decades from now, if the sea level rises as most scientists expect it to, fresh, high-quality drinking water will be much more (i) __________ within 60 miles of the ocean than it is now. Seawater will have (ii) __________ coastal rivers, lakes, and groundwater, turning (iii) __________ water into brackish water.A few decades from now, if the sea level rises as most scientists expect it to, fresh, high-quality drinking water will be much more (i) __________ within 60 miles of the ocean than it is now. Seawater will have (ii) __________ coastal rivers, lakes, and groundwater, turning (iii) __________ water into brackish water.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　scarce; tainted; potable
deplete
deplete: standard unit headword
#422 TC DOUBLE
Memoirs are inherently (i)__________, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)__________: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.Memoirs are inherently (i)__________, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)__________: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　wistful; valedictory
noteserve
note: standard unit headword | serve: standard unit headword
#423 TC TRIPLE
Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　an impersonal; irrelevant to; inseparable from
insulate
insulate: standard unit headword
#424 TC DOUBLE
Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　managing; solving
contributemitigate
contribute: standard unit headword | mitigate: standard unit headword
#425 TC TRIPLE
In a review of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.In a review of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　physical activity; detract from; augmented
contributenotecounteract
contribute: standard unit headword | note: standard unit headword | counteract: standard unit headword
#426 TC DOUBLE
Dismissing the argument that the author's intent should be (i) __________ any interpretation of a literary work, the critic contended that while authorial intentions were (ii) __________ to some degree, they should not necessarily have primacy in every case.Dismissing the argument that the author's intent should be (i) __________ any interpretation of a literary work, the critic contended that while authorial intentions were (ii) __________ to some degree, they should not necessarily have primacy in every case.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　paramount in; germane
notecase
note: standard unit headword | case: standard unit headword
#427 TC TRIPLE
There is (i)__________ evidence that the giant African land snail a. fulica is a serious threat of the spread of human disease. Giant snails do carry rat lungworm—infection by which is a common cause of meningitis—but so do many other snail species. Moreover, if the threat posed by a. fulica were (ii)__________, one would expect to see an increase in disease rates (iii)__________ a rise in the spread of snails. However, in places like New Caledonia, explosions in giant snail populations have coincided with a fall in the number of meningitis cases.There is (i)__________ evidence that the giant African land snail a. fulica is a serious threat of the spread of human disease. Giant snails do carry rat lungworm—infection by which is a common cause of meningitis—but so do many other snail species. Moreover, if the threat posed by a. fulica were (ii)__________, one would expect to see an increase in disease rates (iii)__________ a rise in the spread of snails. However, in places like New Caledonia, explosions in giant snail populations have coincided with a fall in the number of meningitis cases.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　little; major; corresponding to
caseambiguous
case: standard unit headword | ambiguous: standard unit headword
#428 TC TRIPLE
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky's description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky's description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; accurate; impugned
indictcase
indict: standard unit headword | case: standard unit headword

However, in places like New Caledonia, explosions in giant snail populations have coincided with a fall in the number of meningitis cases.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　little; major; corresponding to
